content.question: To what degree do early-selection tracks causally drive segregation and inequality outcomes, versus serving as indicators of pre-existing demand for selective education driven by inequality in school quality?
content.reduction_path: Conduct quasi-experimental analysis comparing cohorts exposed to different admissions rules (e.g., time-series variation in exam difficulty or capacity constraints) to isolate the causal effect of selection mechanism from parental demand; or conduct international comparative analysis of selection-system reform in neighboring countries (Czech Republic, Slovakia) to observe outcome changes.
